latest.title: Progress towards Mining at Northern Zone
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9801M
Oracle Power PLC
20 July 2026
20 July 2026
Oracle Power PLC
(
"
Oracle
"
or the
"
Company
")
Progress
towards Mining at
Northern Zone -
Kalgoorlie Gold Project
Oracle Power PLC (AIM:ORCP), an international project developer, is pleased to
provide an update on progress of the
Native Vegetation Clearing Permit (NVCP) and the Mine Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP), covering the Northern Zone Gold Project (M25/389), part of the Company's Kalgoorlie Gold Project, which is located 25 km east of Kalgoorlie in Western Australia (
see Figure 1
).
Highlights
·
Site clearance permit and Native Vegetation Clearing Permit (NVCP) applications due for submission this month
o
Fauna Report: preparation of final report.
o
Flora Report: preparation of final integrated flora/vegetation report.
·
Mine Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP) progressing
o
Heritage Report: work area clearance survey completed, with preparation of final report.
o
Hydrogeology Report: Nearing completion. Pending flood model to determine the topographic boundary for placing the topsoil.
o
Geotech, Geology and Soil: Laboratory test results received and analysis/report in progress
Naheed Memon, CEO of Oracle, commented
:
"The team has been working in parallel to the recent granting of M25/389 to progress all the inputs required for the submission of the NVCP and MDCP, which will then provide the operating permit for the start of mining operations This work has been progressing well. A significant number of the major inputs for the submission of the NVCP have now been completed, with final deliverables (reports) nearing completion for submission. The MDCP submission will follow as we finalise inputs. We continue to get much closer to turning dirt at Northern Zone and I look forward to updating shareholders on progress as we move forward."
For further information on Oracle, visit the Company
'
s website at
http://www.oraclepower.co.uk
or contact
:
Oracle Power PLC
Naheed Memon - CEO +44 (0) 203 580 4314
Strand Hanson Limited (Nominated Adviser & Broker)
Rory Murphy, Matthew Chandler, Rob Patrick +44 (0) 20 7409 3494
St Brides Partners Limited (Financial PR)
Susie Geliher, Isabel De Salis +44 (0) 20 7236 1177
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of EU Regulation No. 596/2014, which forms part of United Kingdom domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended by virtue of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019.
Figure 1:
Location of
Northern Zone Project showing proximity to the Kalgoorlie "Super Pit", Golden Mile/Fimiston.

latest.title: Placing to Raise £500,000
latest.content:
Today 07:28
RNS Number : 9530K
Oracle Power PLC
03 July 2026
3 July 2026
Oracle Power PLC
("Oracle", the "Company" or the "Group")
Placing to Raise £500,000
Oracle Power PLC (AIM: ORCP), the international project developer, is pleased to announce that it has raised gross proceeds of
£500,000
by way of a placing of
1,250,000,000
new ordinary shares in the capital of the Company (the "Placing Shares") at a price of
0.04
pence per share (the "Placing Price"), representing a 20% discount to the closing mid
-market closing
price on
2
July 2026 (the "Placing"). This Placing
price is
186%
higher than the
Company's
last placing
completed
in August 2025.
Use of Proceeds
The net proceeds of the Placing will be primarily used to progress the development of the projects in Australia, particularly as we now aim to advance swiftly towards gold production following the granting of the mining lease for the Northern Zone. Funds will also be used to advance our projects in Pakistan, as well as for general working capital purposes.
Admission to Trading and Total Voting Rights
Application will be made for the Placing Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM (
"
Admission
"
). It is expected that Admission will become effective at 8:00 a.m. on or
around 9 July 2026. The Placing
Shares will rank
pari passu
with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will be
16,971,394,613 Ordinary Shares, with no shares held in Treasury. Therefore, the total current voting rights in the
Company following Admission will
be 16,971,394,613 and
this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
CMC Markets UK Plc trading as CMC CapX, acted as the Company's placing agent in respect of the Placing.
Naheed Memon, CEO of Oracle, commented:
"
T
he grant
ing
of the
mining lease for
our
Northern Zone
Gold Project
represents a key milestone for our exciting gold project that is
located
close to Kalgoorlie. To finally have this mining lease formally granted is a tremendous result for the Company, our JV partner, Riversgold Limited and our funding and production partner.
We are also progressing well with the development of our energy projects in Pakistan. This fundraise will provide us with additional funds to accelerate development activities until we are able to generate revenue from our gold activities."
*ENDS*
For further information:
Oracle Power PLC
Naheed Memon - CEO
+44 (0) 203 580 4314
Strand Hanson Limited (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Rory Murphy, Matthew Chandler, Rob Patrick
+44 (0) 20 7409 3494
St Brides Partners Limited (Financial PR)
Susie Geliher, Isabel de Salis
+44 (0) 20 7236 1177
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 as it forms part of United Kingdom domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended by virtue of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019.
About Oracle Power PLC:
Oracle Power PLC is an international project developer quoted on London's AIM market. The Company currently has two areas of focus: Western Australia and Pakistan. The Company is active in the energy industry in Pakistan and is working to establish one of the largest green hydrogen production facilities in the region.

latest.title: Mining Lease Granted - Northern Zone Gold Project
latest.content:
Today 12:34
RNS Number : 8248K
Oracle Power PLC
02 July 2026
2 July 2026
Oracle Power PLC
(
"
Oracle
"
or the
"
Company
")
Mining Lease Granted
for Northern Zone
Gold Project
Oracle Power PLC (AIM:ORCP), an international project developer,
is pleased to
announce that Mining Lease M25/389, which covers its Northern Zone Gold Project ("Northern Zone"), has now been granted.
Northern Zone is located 25km east of Kalgoorlie in Western Australia (
see Figure 1
).
The Mining Lease has been granted for a term of 21 years to 01/07/2047 and is renewable thereafter.
Highlights
:
·
Mining Lease M25/389 has now been granted for a term of 21 years and is renewable thereafter
·
Major milestone met for funding and development partnership to proceed with Mega Resources Pty Limited ("MEGA Resources" or "MEGA")
[1]
·
Site clearance application, National Vegetation Clearing Permit (NVCP) due for submission this month
·
Company now focusing on completing the inputs for the requisite Mine Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP) and its submission to Government
Naheed Memon, CEO of Oracle, commented
:
"This represents a key milestone for our very exciting gold project that is so close to Kalgoorlie. To finally have this mining lease formally granted after an exhaustive 18-month process is a tremendous result for the Company, our JV partner, Riversgold Limited ("Riversgold") and our funding and production partner - MEGA Resources.
"Not only does the grant of the mining lease secure long-term tenure, but it also provides us with the ability to submit the NVCP and MDCP. The MDCP team that includes Resources WA and MEGA Resources have been working in parallel to the grant application for M25/389, in order to progress all the inputs required for the submission of the MDCP which will then lead to the operating permit for the start of mining operations.
"A significant number of the inputs have already been completed, such as flora and fauna surveys, hydrogeological wells and testing and heritage surveys with the last of the inputs currently being finalised. I look forward to updating shareholders further in the near term."
On 30 September 2025, Oracle and Riversgold announced that a Mining and Co-Operation agreement had been signed with MEGA Resources to fully fund operations with the project owners to receive 50% of the profits. The grant of Mining Licence M25/389 is highly significant with respect to meeting a key condition precedent in the MEGA Resources agreement.
In summary, as announced previously:
·
Riversgold had signed, on the Project Owners' behalf, a Right to Mine and Co-Operation Agreement with established WA Goldfields mining services provider MEGA Resources (MEGA) - for full project funding, mining, and haulage services at the Northern Zone Gold Project which is located 25km from Kalgoorlie.
·
MEGA is a full-service mining contractor that will be providing all of the funding for the operation; MEGA will also provide geological and engineering services and manage project approvals.
·
The Northern Zone Gold Project near Kalgoorlie is owned 80% by Riversgold and 20% by Oracle (the "Project Owners"). No upfront funding is required from the Project Owners.
·
MEGA will share profits equally (50/50) from operations with the Project Owners.
·
10% of the project's profits generated monthly (funded on a 50/50 basis) will be reinvested back into expansion grade control and step out drilling.
·
Northern Zone Gold Project drilling has already identified a gold mineralised porphyry around 600m wide and around 500m deep from diamond drilling.
·
Conditions Precedent: The MEGA agreement is subject to the conversion of P26/2651 into mining lease M25/389, the execution of a formal Mining Services Agreement which will include a schedule of rates and receipt of any third-party approvals.
For further information on Oracle, visit the Company
'
s website at
http://www.oraclepower.co.uk
or contact
:
Oracle Power PLC
Naheed Memon - CEO +44 (0) 203 580 4314
Strand Hanson Limited (Nominated Adviser & Broker)
Rory Murphy, Matthew Chandler, Rob Patrick +44 (0) 20 7409 3494
St Brides Partners Limited (Financial PR)
Susie Geliher, Isabel de Salis +44 (0) 20 7236 1177
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of EU Regulation No. 596/2014, which forms part of United Kingdom domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended by virtue of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019.
Figure 1
:
Location of
Northern Zone Project showing proximity to the Kalgoorlie "Super Pit", Golden Mile/Fimiston
.
[1]
Announcement dated 30 September 2025 - " Riversgold Signs Mining and Co-Operation Agreement for
Northern Zone Gold Project Near Kalgoorlie - MEGA Resources will fully fund the operations with Project Owners to receive 50%
of the profit

company_30s.what_they_do: ORCP is an Australian exploration company focused on gold and copper deposits. Its flagship sites are the Northern Zone in Western Australia and the Kalgoorlie Gold Project, where it is drilling for high‑grade ore.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits on a region that has historically produced large gold finds. If the assays confirm high gold grades, it could unlock a sizeable reserve that would be attractive to larger miners and could help drive a broader gold market rally.
company_30s.current_state: ORCP is still in the exploration phase, with no revenue yet. It has a small cash balance that is being stretched by drilling costs, but recent assay results show promising gold grades. The share price is trading in the penny range, reflecting the high risk and long time‑to‑revenue profile.

rns_narrative: The company has been releasing a steady stream of exploration updates: recent assays report up to 71 g/t gold, geotechnical drilling is underway, and a mining licence hearing is scheduled for 5 June. These events suggest the company is moving from data collection to regulatory approval and potential financing.

Two themes emerge: (1) Exploration momentum – positive assay results and ongoing drilling indicate a potential resource expansion; (2) Regulatory & financing – the licence hearing and past funding rounds show the company is actively seeking the capital needed to move forward.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of bullish posts about the assay results and a few bearish comments on project delays.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders points to the 71 g/t assay as a sign that the Northern Zone could be a high‑grade find, and they anticipate a funding raise or partnership soon.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the project is still far from commercial and that the licence hearing could be denied, which would leave the company with no clear path to cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has stayed flat; there is no growing optimism or panic, which is typical for a deep‑crashed, low‑volume play.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for ORCP is flat – the stock has no public search interest, signalling maximum surprise potential when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The 5 June mining licence hearing – approval would remove a regulatory hurdle and could trigger a funding round.
An official assay release from the Northern Zone drilling – a positive result could validate the high‑grade claim.
A potential partnership or funding announcement – any capital raise would demonstrate market confidence.
A breakout above the 0.06p support level – a volume‑driven move could signal the start of a mean‑reversion rally.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in daily volume, a clear price move above 0.06p, or a news release confirming licence approval or a new assay. A lack of any of these in the next 60–90 days would be a red flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is deep in a crash‑recovery cycle – the price has already fallen far below its historical lows, and the APEX score suggests a tight squeeze. Confirmation of mean reversion will likely come from a catalyst that lifts investor confidence, after which a gradual climb is expected.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: ORCP has fallen more than 70% from its peak, a classic set‑up for a high‑reward bounce. The recent high‑grade assays give the company a tangible asset that could justify a valuation jump. If the licence is approved or a funding round closes, the price could double or triple in the next 6–12 months, delivering an upside of 200–300% for a small stake.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company remains exploration‑only, with no revenue and a thin cash buffer. If the licence hearing is denied, drilling fails, or a funding round stalls, the stock could collapse back to zero. The worst‑case downside is essentially 100% of the invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100% of the current penny price. Potential gain: 200–300% if a catalyst triggers a bounce to 0.10–0.15p. The asymmetry is roughly 1:3 to 1:5, favouring a long position if a catalyst materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume spike that accompanies a positive assay or licence approval. Set a stop 30–50 pips below the entry (around 0.04p). Target 2× the entry price (≈0.10p). Size the position so that a 30‑50 pips move would not wipe out your capital.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position (or a cash‑only approach) until the licence hearing or an assay is released. If the price breaks 0.06p on high volume, consider a larger entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there is no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk. The only terminal risk is the company’s inability to secure funding or clear regulatory hurdles.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ORCP is a deep‑crashed exploration play with silent chatter and a clear catalyst window; a positive assay or licence approval could unlock a 200–300% upside, while the downside is limited to the current penny price.
